question: The latest archaeological findings ______ the theory that an advanced civilization existed here thousands of years ago.
explanation:
• **Bear out**: To confirm or support evidence. ✅
• **Bear up**: To remain brave in tough times. 💪

question: The speaker's provocative comments ______ a heated debate among the audience members.
explanation:
• **Called forth**: To elicit a specific reaction. 📣
• **Called off**: To cancel. ❌

question: After losing his job, he had to ______ for a new source of income to support his family.
explanation:
• **Cast about**: To search or look around for something. 🔍
• **Cast down**: To be depressed. 😔

question: The government tried to ______ the growing public anger by announcing a series of welfare measures.
explanation:
• **Damp down**: To subdue or reduce intensity. 💧
• **Damp off**: A plant disease term. 🌱

question: During the crisis, the leader had to ______ all his years of experience to navigate the party safely.
explanation:
• **Draw on**: To utilize a resource or quality. 🏦
• **Draw up**: To prepare a document. 📄

question: He was ______ by his friends to participate in the dangerous stunt, which eventually led to an injury.
explanation:
• **Egged on**: To incite or urge someone to do something foolish. 🥚
• **Others**: Non-standard phrasal verbs. ❌

question: The CEO successfully ______ the hostile takeover bid by presenting a better plan to the shareholders.
explanation:
• **Fended off**: To defend against an attack or criticism. 🤺
• **Fended for**: To take care of oneself. 🏠

question: The official report tended to ______ the corruption allegations to protect the department's reputation.
explanation:
• **Gloss over**: To ignore or avoid dealing with unpleasant facts. 🌫️
• **Others**: Non-standard usage. ❌

question: After weeks of intense debate, the diplomats finally managed to ______ a peace treaty.
explanation:
• **Hammer out**: To reach an agreement after long discussion. 🔨
• **Hammer away**: To work continuously at something. 👷

question: The new design of the car ______ to the classic models of the 1960s.
explanation:
• **Harks back**: To remember or refer to something in the past. 🔙
• **Others**: Incorrect prepositions. ❌

question: The lake began to ______ as the temperature dropped below zero degrees Celsius.
explanation:
• **Ice over**: To become covered with ice. ❄️
• **Ice up**: To be blocked with ice (like a pipe). 🚿

question: The company decided to ______ the loss-making project and focus on its core strengths.
explanation:
• **Jettison off**: To discard or abandon something. 🚀
• **Jump at**: To accept eagerly. 🤩

question: The witness was accused of ______ vital information from the investigative team.
explanation:
• **Keeping back**: To withhold information. ✋
• **Keeping up**: To maintain speed. 🏃

question: The judge is expected to ______ the law regarding environmental protection in his next ruling.
explanation:
• **Lay down**: To officially establish a rule or law. ⚖️
• **Lay off**: To dismiss employees. 💼

question: Despite his talent, the young musician failed to ______ to the high standards set by his predecessors.
explanation:
• **Measure up**: To reach a required standard. 📏
• **Measure out**: To apportion. ⚖️

question: He was so exhausted that he ______ right in the middle of the boring meeting.
explanation:
• **Nodded off**: To fall asleep unintentionally. 😴
• **Others**: Incorrect usage. ❌

question: The politician finally decided to ______ to his mistakes during the live television interview.
explanation:
• **Own up**: To confess or admit fault. 🗣️
• **Others**: Non-standard phrasal verbs. ❌

question: The government has decided to ______ the use of plastic bags over the next two years.
explanation:
• **Phase out**: To gradually discontinue. 📉
• **Phase in**: To gradually introduce. 📈

question: No one believed he would survive the accident, but he managed to ______ miraculously.
explanation:
• **Pull through**: To survive a dangerous situation/illness. 🏥
• **Pull off**: To succeed in a difficult task. ✅

question: The noisy crowd began to ______ as the national anthem started playing.
explanation:
• **Quiet down**: To become silent. 🤫
• **Others**: Incorrect prepositions. ❌

question: The opposition party tried to ______ the old scandals to damage the government's image.
explanation:
• **Rake up**: To bring up unpleasant past memories. 🧹
• **Rake in**: To earn a lot of money. 💵

question: He decided to ______ a new project with great enthusiasm and dedication.
explanation:
• **Set about**: To start doing something with determination. 🚀
• **Set up**: To establish. 🏗️

question: The central bank had to ______ the falling currency to prevent an economic crisis.
explanation:
• **Shore up**: To support or strengthen something weak. 🧱
• **Others**: Non-standard phrasal verbs. ❌

question: The demand for winter clothes began to ______ as the summer approached.
explanation:
• **Taper off**: To diminish gradually. 📉
• **Take off**: To depart. 🛫

question: The invention of the steam engine ______ a new era of industrialization.
explanation:
• **Ushered in**: To herald or start a new era. 🛎️
• **Used up**: To consume. 🪫

question: The designer decided to ______ the old apartment to give it a modern and stylish look.
explanation:
• **Vamp up**: To improve appearance. 💅
• **Vouch for**: To guarantee. 📜

question: The sailors had to ______ the heavy storm in a small, unstable boat.
explanation:
• **Wait out**: To wait until an unpleasant event stops. 🕒
• **Wait on**: To serve. 🍽️

question: The editor began to ______ the unnecessary words to make the article more concise.
explanation:
• **X out**: To delete or cross out. ✖️
• **Others**: Non-standard phrasal verbs. ❌

question: The suspect was forced to ______ his passport to the investigative agency.
explanation:
• **Yield up**: To relinquish or give up possession. 🤲
• **Yield to**: To surrender to pressure. 🙇

question: The investigative agency began to ______ the specific location of the hidden bunker.
explanation:
• **Zero in on**: To focus all attention on a particular thing. 🎯
